layman_headline: Market fear is rising after U.S.-Iran tensions — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market sold off sharply today, with Bitcoin dropping below $63,000 after news of a U.S. military strike on Iran and uncertainty around Trump's China comments. Almost every coin is deep in the red, with some altcoins down 10-18% in a week. The Fear & Greed Index sits at 27 ('Fear'), which is improving slightly but still signals caution. While fear often creates the best buying opportunities, the selling pressure hasn't shown signs of exhausting yet.
layman_action: Wait on the sidelines for now. The best candidate is Hyperliquid (HYPE), which has the strongest chart structure among the watchlist, but it just dropped 11% today and needs to find a floor before entering. Watch for stabilization around $55-57 before considering a buy.
expert_reasoning: The macro backdrop is hostile: U.S. strikes on Iran pushed BTC below $63K, BTC dominance is elevated at 56.1%, and Fear & Greed is at 27. High BTC dominance is a headwind for alts. HYPE stands out with a +1.10 chart bias score and bullish marubozu candle, but the -11.35% 24h drop signals capitulation selling that may not be done. RSI at 59 is not yet oversold — ideally we'd want RSI near 35-40 for a high-conviction swing entry. LDO has an exceptional 6.51x risk/reward ratio and +18% weekly move suggesting momentum, but it's below all MAs with bearish structure. ETH at RSI 35 is interesting but lacks catalyst and has poor risk/reward to nearest resistance. The best play is to wait for HYPE to find support near $55 where risk/reward improves substantially.
risk_factors: Geopolitical escalation (U.S.-Iran conflict) could trigger further broad crypto selloffs
BTC dominance at 56.1% creates persistent headwinds for altcoin recovery
HYPE's 11% daily drop may signal the start of a deeper correction rather than a dip to buy
watch_list: HYPE price action around $55-57 support zone — stabilization here would trigger a BUY signal
BTC holding $60,000 as macro support — a break below would signal deeper market correction
Fear & Greed Index trajectory — a move below 20 would signal extreme fear and better contrarian entry
market_context: Broad risk-off selloff driven by U.S. strike on Iran and Trump China uncertainty. BTC dominance is high at 56.1%, punishing alts, while Fear & Greed at 27 is improving but not yet at extreme levels that historically mark bottoms.
context_banner: Geopolitical shock has triggered a market-wide selloff — disciplined traders wait for the dust to settle before deploying capital into strong setups.

key_signals.description: Bitcoin dropped below $63K after U.S. strike on Iran and Trump China comments, creating broad market risk-off sentiment that invalidates most swing entries.
Fear & Greed at 27 (Fear) is improving from 25 yesterday, but not yet at extreme levels (sub-20) that historically mark reliable bottoms for contrarian entries.
HYPE RSI at 59 is not yet oversold — our most reliable signal source (80% win rate) suggests waiting for RSI to drop to 35-40 range for higher probability entries.
HYPE has the strongest chart bias (+1.10) with bullish marubozu and above-MA structure, making it the top candidate once selling pressure exhausts.
Bitcoin liquidity cluster analysis and futures flow coverage suggests institutional positioning is driving price action, with key support levels being tested.
